laws.title: Minnesota Earned Sick and Safe Time — Statewide Law
laws.summary: Minnesota's Earned Sick and Safe Time (ESST) law requires all employers (including those with 1 employee) to provide paid sick and safe time. Employees accrue 1 hour per 30 hours worked, up to 48 hours per year. Leave can be used for employee illness, family care, domestic violence, and public health emergencies. Unused leave carries over.
compliance_actions: Provide ESST to all Minnesota employees starting accrual January 1, 2024
Track accrual at 1 hour per 30 hours worked (up to 48 hours/year)
Allow carryover of unused ESST (up to 48 hours)
Update your employee handbook with ESST policy
Post the required ESST notice and include it in offer letters/onboarding materials

laws.title: Minnesota Non-Compete Ban (— All Non-Competes Void for New Hires
laws.summary: Minnesota banned non-compete agreements for employees and independent contractors effective July 1, 2023. Any non-compete signed after this date is void and unenforceable. Non-solicitation and non-disclosure agreements remain enforceable. Existing non-competes signed before July 1, 2023 may still be enforceable.
compliance_actions: Stop using non-compete agreements for Minnesota employees immediately — they are void after July 1, 2023
Review all offer letters and employment agreements for Minnesota employees — remove non-compete clauses
Replace non-competes with enforceable alternatives: NDAs, non-solicitation agreements, and IP assignment agreements
Pre-July 2023 non-competes may still be valid — consult counsel before attempting to enforce

laws.title: Minnesota Pat†eť Transparency — Salary Ranges in Job Postings (Jan 2025)
laws.summary: Minnesota employers with 30+ employees must include the starting salary or hourly wage range in job postings, along with a general description of benefits. Applies to positions that will be or may be performed in Minnesota, including remote roles.
compliance_actions: Include salary ranges in all Minnesota job postings (internal and external)
Include a description of benefits (health insurance, PTO, retirement, etc.) in job postings
Apply to remote roles that could be filled by Minnesota residents
Train recruiters and HR on the new requirements
